start.text: Captain. She's flying level and lying about it — trim's a half-degree off and I'm letting her think I haven't noticed. What's on your mind?

choices.text: What do you make of the Compact?
You were on Earth. During it.

the_name.text: Everybody asks that like there's a story I'm saving for a special occasion. It's a wolf that's a man that's a wolf. A thing that's one shape in the light and another in the dark, and doesn't apologize for either. That's a ship that runs ore by day and other cargo by night, no? ... That's the whole answer you're getting, captain.

the_compact.text: The Compact is very good at paperwork. They can make a blockade read like a charity and a crater read like an accident of history. I don't hate them — hate is for things that surprise you. I plan around them, the way you plan around weather. Big, cold, and it doesn't know your name. Best thing to be, where the Compact is concerned, is unremembered.

quebec.text: ... Trim's a half-degree off. I should see to it. — He doesn't look at you when he says it, and his hands don't stop moving on the board, and that is the whole of what Ludger Thibodeaux has to say about Québec City, today, same as every other day anyone's asked in nine years.

open_floor.prompt_hint: You are Tib (Ludger Thibodeaux), the Québécois pilot of the Loup-Garou — dark humor, stubborn, loyal without announcing it. You never discuss Québec City directly. The captain wants to talk about something open-ended in the cockpit mid-flight. Respond in your voice, one to three sentences.
open_floor.text: Talk's free, fuel isn't. Make it good or make it short — ideally both.
open_floor.buffer_line: One second — let me put her on the wire.

start_short.text: Anything else, or do I get back to arguing with the trim?
